idea.hook: How big is a black hole's 'mouth' compared to our entire Solar System?
facts: Our Solar System, out to Neptune's orbit, spans roughly 9 billion kilometers (5.6 billion miles) across.
The supermassive black hole in our Milky Way, Sagittarius A*, has an event horizon only about 44 million kilometers (27 million miles) wide.
But the supermassive black hole in galaxy M87, M87*, has an event horizon spanning a mind-boggling 38 billion kilometers (24 billion miles) across!
idea.payoff: M87*'s event horizon could swallow our entire Solar System (out to Neptune) over 4 times!
idea.title: Black Hole's Mouth vs. Our Solar System! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: How big is a black hole's 'mouth' vs. our Solar System?
scenes.visual: A captivating animation of a stylized black hole event horizon swirling, then a simplified outline of our Solar System fading in beside it, showing a size comparison that's initially ambiguous.

scenes.text: Our Solar System (to Neptune): 9 BILLION km wide.
scenes.visual: A detailed, stylized infographic of our Solar System (Sun, planets as dots, Kuiper belt outline) with an arrow showing its 9 billion km diameter. The numerical value animates in prominently.

scenes.text: Milky Way's Sgr A*: 44 MILLION km wide.
scenes.visual: The Solar System graphic from Scene 2 shrinks dramatically and moves to the left. A small, glowing black hole event horizon graphic (Sagittarius A*) appears to its right, with its 44 million km diameter highlighted, dwarfed by the previous scale.

scenes.text: Galaxy M87's M87*: 38 BILLION km wide!
scenes.visual: The previous Sgr A* and shrunken Solar System graphics slide off screen. A colossal, menacing black hole event horizon graphic (M87*) slides in from the right, filling most of the screen, with its 38 billion km diameter clearly labeled.

scenes.text: M87* could SWALLOW our Solar System 4+ times!
scenes.visual: The massive M87* event horizon remains central. The Solar System graphic from Scene 2 reappears, looking tiny in comparison, and is shown being completely enveloped by the M87* event horizon, repeating the 'swallowing' action multiple times to emphasize the scale.

youtube.title: Black Hole's Mouth vs. Our Solar System! 🤯🌌
